Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. I Built an Open-World Engine for the N64 [video] 155 upvotes, 15 comments
  2. Desperately Seeking Space Friends 29 upvotes, 2 comments
  3. Paper Tape Is All You Need – Training a Transformer on a 1976 Minicomputer 73 upvotes, 10 comments
  4. Nashville library launches Memory Lab for digitizing home movies 175 upvotes, 42 comments
  5. Matadisco – Decentralized Data Discovery 36 upvotes, 5 comments
  6. I put all 8,642 Spanish laws in Git – every reform is a commit 442 upvotes, 138 comments
  7. The bee that everyone wants to save 191 upvotes, 63 comments
  8. C++26: A User-Friednly assert() macro 13 upvotes, 1 comments
  9. Cocoa-Way – Native macOS Wayland compositor for running Linux apps seamlessly 181 upvotes, 63 comments
  10. Anatomy of the .claude/ folder 548 upvotes, 236 comments
  11. Arm releases first in-house chip, with Meta as debut customer 58 upvotes, 19 comments
  12. LG's new 1Hz display is the secret behind a new laptop's battery life 287 upvotes, 147 comments
  13. AMD's Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 Dual Edition crams 208MB of cache into a single chip 237 upvotes, 122 comments
  14. Go Naming Conventions: A Practical Guide 52 upvotes, 20 comments
  15. CERN uses tiny AI models burned into silicon for real-time LHC data filtering 199 upvotes, 101 comments
  16. Go hard on agents, not on your filesystem 480 upvotes, 275 comments
  17. Gerard of Cremona 14 upvotes, 3 comments
  18. Improved Git Diffs with Delta, Fzf and a Little Shell Scripting 33 upvotes, 12 comments
  19. Show HN: Twitch Roulette – Find live streamers who need views the most 162 upvotes, 90 comments
  20. Make macOS consistently bad unironically 470 upvotes, 325 comments
  21. Folk are getting dangerously attached to AI that always tells them they're right 66 upvotes, 35 comments
  22. Britain today generating 90%+ of electricity from renewables 282 upvotes, 190 comments
  23. A single-file C allocator with explicit heaps and tuning knobs 38 upvotes, 20 comments
  24. Behind the Scenes of the Westworld UI 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  25. AI chatbots are "Yes-Men" that reinforce bad relationship decisions, study finds 157 upvotes, 121 comments
  26. ‘Energy independence feels practical’: Europeans building mini solar farms 332 upvotes, 307 comments
  27. Iran-linked hackers breach FBI director's personal email 347 upvotes, 464 comments
  28. The risk of AI isn't making us lazy, but making "lazy" look productive 8 upvotes, 7 comments
  29. We built a multi-agent research hub. The waitlist is a reverse-CAPTCHA 3 upvotes, 1 comments
  30. Toma (YC W24) is hiring a Senior/Staff Eng to build AI automotive coworkers 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 12:00am, 29 March 2026. Source here.
